title: Availability, Disruption Budgets, Graceful Draining, Autoscaling, and Capacity · API Object Lens: K8s Vertical Node Autoscale Boundaries
summary: A two-zone service scales from noisy metrics, violates its recovery target during drain, and has enough replicas but insufficient failure-domain and resource headroom. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s vertical node autoscale boundaries increment in availability and autoscaling operating policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare vertical resource recommendation or mutation, in-place resize support, node autoscaling, workload disruption, scheduling, quotas, and provider dependencies.
Derive HPA desired replicas from requests, utilization, custom or external metrics, missing samples, tolerance, stabilization, and scale policies.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio

version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example service has 4 replicas but all share one zone, a strict disruption budget blocks drain, and HPA oscillates because requests and metrics are wrong. Balance failure domains, PDB, rollout surge, autoscaling signals, stabilization, and capacity. Competency probe: Compare vertical resource recommendation or mutation, in-place resize support, node autoscaling, workload disruption, scheduling, quotas, and provider dependencies. A defensible solution applies K8s Vertical Node Autoscale Boundaries, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to availability and autoscaling operating policy.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-availability-disruption-autoscaling-3
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-availability-disruption-autoscaling-3
    artifact: availability-policy.yaml
    decision: worked k8s availability disruption autoscaling 3
    environment: shared multi-tenant cluster under Restricted Pod Security
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      objective: 99.9-percent-availability
      replicas: 4-across-2-zones
      pdb: minAvailable-3
      hpa: autoscaling-v2-cpu-and-queue
      stabilization: bounded
      node-headroom: measured
      faults: drain-zone-loss-metrics-gap
    verify: kubectl get deployment,pdb,hpa -n intake -o json
    failure: Reject replica-count-only HA, PDB as involuntary-failure protection, limit-based HPA math, or autoscalers without capacity and feedback analysis.
    repair: Tie replicas and topology to objectives, calculate disruption and scaling, reserve headroom, inject lag and failure, and measure user-visible service.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-vertical-node-autoscale-boundaries-17h1pg
  - competency: k8s-vertical-node-autoscale-boundaries
    artifact: availability-policy.yaml
    decision: vertical node autoscale boundaries
    environment: rootless development cluster with an empty namespace
    fault-injection: remove DNS egress from an otherwise ready workload
    retained-proof: bound claim snapshot checksum and application consistency query
    case-key: 17h1pg
    change: |
      objective: 99.9-percent-availability
      replicas: 4-across-2-zones
      pdb: minAvailable-3
      hpa: autoscaling-v2-cpu-and-queue
      stabilization: bounded
      node-headroom: measured
      faults: drain-zone-loss-metrics-gap
    verify: kubectl get deployment,pdb,hpa -n intake -o json
    failure: Reject replica-count-only HA, PDB as involuntary-failure protection, limit-based HPA math, or autoscalers without capacity and feedback analysis.
    repair: Tie replicas and topology to objectives, calculate disruption and scaling, reserve headroom, inject lag and failure, and measure user-visible service.
